ux_issues.description: Day 7 is overloaded with both Chinchero (Sacred Valley) activities AND the full Machu Picchu day trip (early train + citadel + return train). This creates a physically impossible schedule. The AI should have placed Machu Picchu on a separate day (Day 8) after Ollantaytambo as requested.
ux_issues.suggestion: When appending itinerary items to an existing day that already has a full schedule, the AI should check for time conflicts or create a new day instead.

ux_issues.description: The two-week Peru trip only has 9 days of itinerary items. Lima (2 days at start + 2 days at end) was never prompted for in the 6 turns, but the AI also didn't proactively suggest filling those gaps when asked for a 'final complete summary' in Turn 6.
ux_issues.suggestion: When summarizing a trip that has unfilled days relative to the stated duration, the AI should note the gaps and offer to fill them.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Turn 1 had 4 create_itinerary_items tool calls — the AI called the tool multiple times with overlapping items, resulting in 3 calls being rejected as duplicates. This is wasteful but not harmful since the dedup guard caught it.
ai_behavior_issues.context: First tool call created 16 items. Subsequent 3 calls attempted to re-add the same items and received 'already_exists' responses.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The fabrication detection system worked flawlessly across all 6 turns. Per-turn item counts: T1=16, T2=30, T3=32, T4=37, T5=47, T6=47 (no change on summary). All 5 content turns (1-5) correctly called create_itinerary_items. Turn 3 appended food items without overwriting (32 >= 30). Turn 6 correctly did NOT call create_itinerary_items for a summary request. No fabrication phrases appeared without a corresponding tool call. The AI was responsive to explicit tool-call instructions. Two fabrication phrases ('added to your itinerary') appeared in Turns 3 and 4, both legitimate since the tool was actually called. Final itinerary has 47 items across 9 days — well above the 15-item minimum. The only quality issue is Day 7 being overloaded with both Chinchero and Machu Picchu activities.
